Labour-run council shoots down claim migrant cultures 'don't respect women'

Olympics star Luana Alonso has been banned from staying at the Olympic Village

INSTAGRAM

ByJack Otway,

Published: 05/08/2024

The Paraguayan recently announced she'd be retiring at the age of 20

Olympics swimmer Luana Alonso has been banned from staying at the Olympic Village after being accused of creating an 'inappropriate environment'.

The Paraguayan recently finished bottom of the women's 100m butterfly heat, where she finished five seconds behind Helena Rosendahl Bach.

Alonso has since said she would be retiring from swimming, having become a popular figure online.Athletes, even once eliminated, are usually able to stay at the Olympic Village in Paris.Olympics star Luana Alonso has been banned from staying at the Olympic Village|INSTAGRAMBut she now won't be able to, with a statement released confirming she's been banned from staying at the village due to her creating an 'inappropriate atmosphere'.A statement from Larissa Schaerer, the head of the COP mission, read: "Her presence is creating an inappropriate atmosphere within Team Paraguay."We thank her for proceeding as instructed, as it was of her own free will that she did not spend the night in the Athletes' Village."Alonso is yet to respond to the allegations.JUST IN:Kishane Thompson 'was robbed' as Olympics fans vent fury after Noah Lyles wins 100m goldNews of the 20-year-old's ban comes shortly after the swimmer had said she would be retiring from the sport.Writing on Instagram, to her 491,000 followers, she wrote: “It's official now!"I'm retiring from swimming, thank you all so much for the support!"Sorry Paraguay, I only have to thank you!”READ MORE:Andy Murray given special present from Adam Peaty at the Olympics as Dan Evans reveals allExplaining her decision further, she said: "I am very emotional because it was my last race. I am retiring from swimming.“I have been swimming for so long, 18 years, and I have so many feelings.“Unfortunately, I made the decision to stop and I am happy that my last race will be at the Olympic Games.”Alonso, who previously competed at the Tokyo Olympics, said she'd made her decision 'a long time ago'.LATEST SPORTS NEWS:Novak Djokovic's inspirational speech leaked after finally winning Olympic gold with Carlos Alcaraz victoryNoah Lyles' girlfriend suffers big backlash after Team USA star wins gold at Paris OlympicsImane Khelif makes feelings clear as controversial Olympics star lashes out amid gender scrutinyOlympics star Luana Alonso is yet to address the statement saying she's been banned from staying at the Olympic Village|GETTYShe said: "People. My decision was made a long time ago! It wasn't because of the Games!“So nothing, I just have to thank everyone who always supported me.”With Alonso's last race ending with the Paraguayan finishing bottom, she also apologised to people in her home country.“It didn’t happen the way I wanted it to," she said while reflecting on her failure to come out on top.Olympics star Luana Alonso recently confirmed she'd be retiring from tennis at the age of 20|INSTAGRAM“I sincerely apologise to Paraguay, but I did what I could, despite several things that happened to me before.“So, I’m happy and also to retire here with a full stadium and finally have the Olympic Games.”

Alonso has since said she would be retiring from swimming, having become a popular figure online.

Athletes, even once eliminated, are usually able to stay at the Olympic Village in Paris.

Olympics star Luana Alonso has been banned from staying at the Olympic Village

INSTAGRAM

But she now won't be able to, with a statement released confirming she's been banned from staying at the village due to her creating an 'inappropriate atmosphere'.

A statement from Larissa Schaerer, the head of the COP mission, read: "Her presence is creating an inappropriate atmosphere within Team Paraguay.

"We thank her for proceeding as instructed, as it was of her own free will that she did not spend the night in the Athletes' Village."

Alonso is yet to respond to the allegations.

JUST IN:Kishane Thompson 'was robbed' as Olympics fans vent fury after Noah Lyles wins 100m gold

News of the 20-year-old's ban comes shortly after the swimmer had said she would be retiring from the sport.

Writing on Instagram, to her 491,000 followers, she wrote: “It's official now!

"I'm retiring from swimming, thank you all so much for the support!

"Sorry Paraguay, I only have to thank you!”

READ MORE:Andy Murray given special present from Adam Peaty at the Olympics as Dan Evans reveals all

Explaining her decision further, she said: "I am very emotional because it was my last race. I am retiring from swimming.

“I have been swimming for so long, 18 years, and I have so many feelings.

“Unfortunately, I made the decision to stop and I am happy that my last race will be at the Olympic Games.”

Alonso, who previously competed at the Tokyo Olympics, said she'd made her decision 'a long time ago'.

LATEST SPORTS NEWS:

Olympics star Luana Alonso is yet to address the statement saying she's been banned from staying at the Olympic Village

GETTY

She said: "People. My decision was made a long time ago! It wasn't because of the Games!

“So nothing, I just have to thank everyone who always supported me.”

With Alonso's last race ending with the Paraguayan finishing bottom, she also apologised to people in her home country.

“It didn’t happen the way I wanted it to," she said while reflecting on her failure to come out on top.

Olympics star Luana Alonso recently confirmed she'd be retiring from tennis at the age of 20

INSTAGRAM

“I sincerely apologise to Paraguay, but I did what I could, despite several things that happened to me before.

“So, I’m happy and also to retire here with a full stadium and finally have the Olympic Games.”
title: Olympics star banned from staying at village for creating 'inappropriate environment' as statement released
description: Olympics swimmer Luana Alonso has been banned from staying at the Olympic Village after being accused of creating an 'inappropriate environment'
